# claudetxt Specification

claudetxt is a command line program implemented in Rust that:
* Takes as input a file generated by Claude Code's `/export` command.
* Outputs a standalone HTML file generated by converting the input file.

Claude Code's `/export` files are structured text that rely heavily on
a few simple conventions, indentation, special unicode characters, and
ASCII-type art. claudetxt transforms that to HTML.

The HTML output needs to be viewable on screen as well as print nicely
to standard US letter paper. Ideally the screen and print output have
similar appearance, i.e., the screen version has bounded geometry.  As
a standalone file the HTML output needs to include all styles.  The
styling must be compatible with weasyprint for generating nicely
paginated PDFs although "Save to PDF" from typical web browsers must
also generate reasonable output.
